author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
The Difficulty of the Distinction Made in the Choice of One and the Rejection of Another.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: The Difficulty of the Distinction Made in the Choice of One and the Rejection of Another.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “But why,” it is said, “in one and the same case, not only of infants, but even of twin children, is the judgment so diverse?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Is it not a similar question, “Why in a different case is the judgment the same?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Let us recall, then, those labourers in the vineyard who worked the whole day, and those who toiled one hour.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Certainly the case was different as to the labour expended, and yet there was the same judgment in paying the wages.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Did the murmurers in this case hear anything from the householder except, Such is my will?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Certainly such was his liberality towards some, that there could be no injustice towards others.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And both these classes, indeed, are among the good.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Nevertheless, so far as it concerns justice and grace, it may be truly said to the guilty who is condemned, also concerning the guilty who is delivered, “Take what thine is, and go thy way;” “I will give unto this one that which is not due;” “Is it not lawful for me to do what I will? is thine eye evil because I am good?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And how if he should say, “Why not to me also?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: He will hear, and with reason, “Who art thou, O man, that repliest against God?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And although assuredly in the one case you see a most benignant benefactor, and in your own case a most righteous exactor, in neither case do you behold an unjust God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For although He would be righteous even if He were to punish both, he who is delivered has good ground for thankfulness, he who is condemned has no ground for finding fault.
